doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_buildopt_decimal_p: build-time predicate reporting whether libmpfr was compiled with decimal-float support (MPFR_WANT_DECIMAL_FLOATS, IEEE 754-2008 decimal32/64/128). C returns int (0 or 1) per a preprocessor branch; TS returns boolean. The TS port has no decimal-float type and the substrate is binary all the way down — so the value is the compile-time constant `false`. The libmpfr-side observable depends on how the system libmpfr was built; the golden_driver emits the TS-expected `false` unconditionally. Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums (happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5) are inapplicable to no-arg accessor functions whose input domain is empty — a single happy case is the entire test surface.
divergence_from_c: C returns int (0 or 1) from a preprocessor branch on MPFR_WANT_DECIMAL_FLOATS; TS returns boolean and is unconditionally `false`. Pure-TS has no IEEE-754-2008 decimal type, so advertising decimal-float support would be a lie. The golden_driver does NOT call libmpfr's mpfr_buildopt_decimal_p() for the expected output (its return depends on the system libmpfr build flags) — it hard-codes `false` to match the TS-port contract.
refs: mpfr/src/buildopt.c L65-L73 — the C reference (mpfr_buildopt_decimal_p preprocessor branch).
CLAUDE.md Rule 7 — tag minimums (carved out here per the spec.doc note above).
CLAUDE.md Rule 12 — pure-TS port has no decimal-float interop on either Bun or Node.
